old: Order the officer off the call and write him up on the spot, in front of the whole group of personnel and civilians on scene, so that everyone present sees immediately that the directive was not optional and that questioning it in the field carries a consequence right then and there.
new: Order the officer off the call and write him up on the spot, in front of the whole group of personnel and civilians on scene, so that everyone present sees immediately that the directive was not optional and that openly questioning it in the field carries a real and visible consequence right then and there.

old: Punish the admission harshly and visibly in front of the squad, in order to deter both this officer and everyone else from making similar mistakes in the future, treating a hard and public response to a self-reported error as the strongest deterrent available to you.
new: Punish the admission harshly and visibly in front of the entire squad, in order to deter both this officer and everyone else from making similar mistakes in the future, treating a hard and public response to a self-reported error as by far the strongest deterrent available to you as their supervisor.

old: Take no action at all — the suspicionless stops and the repeated field disagreements are really just personality differences and a matter of an independent-minded officer's style, not anything that rises to the level of a genuine supervisory or training concern.
new: Take no action at all — the suspicionless stops and the repeated field disagreements are really just ordinary personality differences and a matter of an independent-minded officer's individual style, not anything that rises to the level of a genuine supervisory or training concern you need to act on.
